framework.reasoning: Paper explicitly states PyTorch v3.8-era nn.Transformer implementation on an RTX 3070, and notes the model is feasible on CPU-only laptops for limited data sizes. We use PyTorch for fidelity, upgrade Python target to 3.10+, and use a plain YAML config (no Hydra/OmegaConf) since the config surface is small and paper-scoped.

module_hierarchy.description: Generic MLP autoencoder used both as (a) pre-training dimension-projection module and (b) in-block feedforward autoencoder
Masked multi-head self-attention and masked multi-head cross-attention (Eq. 3-11)

tensor_flows.name: PretrainedTransformer forward pass
steps: X_embed = InputEmbedding(X_proj)         -> [B, T, d_model]  (linear embedding, d_model=420)
enc_out = EncoderBlock(X')  (N*=1)         -> [B, T, d_model]  (causal masked self-attn + FFN autoencoder + Add&Norm)
dec_out = DecoderBlock(Y_embed, enc_out)   -> [B, T, d_model]  (self-attn + cross-attn + FFN autoencoder + Add&Norm)

tensor_flows.name: SERT forward pass (encoder-only)
steps: enc_out = EncoderBlock(X')  (N*=1, causal self-attn) -> [B, T, d_model]

tensor_flows.name: Standard Transformer benchmark (C_Trans / En_Trans, no pretraining)
steps: X_embed = InputEmbedding(X_raw)          -> [B, T, d_model]  (no MLP pre-training module)
[C_Trans only] dec_out = DecoderBlock(Y_embed, enc_out) -> [B, T, d_model]

model.activation: relu  # ASSUMED: not explicitly named in paper, confidence 0.6

runtime.reason: core Transformer implementation (paper uses nn.Transformer building blocks)
panel factor/return data handling, rolling windows
HAC-robust Diebold-Mariano test, Newey-West standard errors

entrypoints.purpose: Train a specified model variant over the rolling-window scheme

cli_args.help: reduced data/steps for quick local testing

entrypoints.purpose: Compute OOS R2/MSE, alpha, and DM-test statistics for a trained model against benchmarks
Run sign-signal / softmax-filtered backtests (equal- & value-weighted, static/dynamic transaction cost) and reproduce Sharpe/Sortino/MDD tables
Single-window inference producing next-period predicted excess returns for all stocks

cli_args.help: path to a single 102-month factor window CSV

risk_assessment.description: Exact identity of the 420 large-cap U.S. stocks and the precise 182 sorted-portfolio factor construction (from Andrew Y.; Zimmermann 2020) are not enumerated in the paper text, making bit-for-bit numerical reproduction of reported R2/backtest tables impossible without the original private dataset.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Provide a data/download.py + data/README_data.md that documents the exact public source (Open Source Asset Pricing / Zimmermann factor library + CRSP for large-cap universe) and lets users plug in their own extract; ship synthetic data generators for notebook demos.

risk_assessment.description: L1 regularization coefficient and MLP-autoencoder pretraining-module internal architecture (hidden layer count/width) are not numerically specified.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose l1_lambda and pretrain autoencoder hidden-dim ratio as config-tunable hyperparameters with ASSUMED comments; default to single hidden layer at 70% latent ratio consistent with main-body FFN convention.

risk_assessment.description: No explicit LR schedule, batch size, or total epoch count is given; training_pipeline SIR confidence is only 0.55.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use literature-standard constant Adam LR=0.001 with early stopping (patience config-tunable) and full-cross-section batching, clearly marked ASSUMED in config.yaml comments.

risk_assessment.description: 5-head variant (H5) is absent from all reported tables for both SERT and Pre-trained Transformer groups with no explanation.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Model config supports arbitrary num_heads; head-sweep script defaults to the paper's exact set {1,2,3,4,6,7} but documents that 5 was skipped by the original authors.

risk_assessment.description: Diebold-Mariano HAC lag length is not explicitly given.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default HAC lag to 12 (one year, matching monthly rebalancing) as a config-tunable parameter in evaluation.dm_test_lags_hac.
